description: 18 highest-severity pain points across all departments at Lakeshore Development. These represent the most impactful operational problems identified through 65+ hours of employee interviews. Full catalog has 97 pain points across 15 departments.

critical_pain_points.name: Triple Data Entry in Scheduling
critical_pain_points.description: Information entered 3 times: Daily Logs + Schedule + PO with the same data. All 7 schedulers suffer this daily.
critical_pain_points.impact: Hours of daily rework for entire scheduling team. Most frequently cited pain point across all interviews.
critical_pain_points.detection_signal: Schedulers spending >30% of their day on data entry instead of coordination
critical_pain_points.solution: Single entry form with automatic propagation to daily log, schedule, and PO

critical_pain_points.name: QuickBooks Class Entry (86 bills = 2h/day)
critical_pain_points.description: Manual classification of 86 bills per day in QuickBooks takes 2 hours. Two slow systems (BT + QB) compounding the problem.
critical_pain_points.impact: 10+ hours per week lost on manual data entry. Scales linearly with company growth.
critical_pain_points.detection_signal: Finance team spending first 2 hours of each day on classification instead of analysis
critical_pain_points.solution: Automatic mapping of project classes between construction management system and QuickBooks

critical_pain_points.name: Invoices Without Purchase Orders
critical_pain_points.description: Supervisors authorize repairs verbally without creating POs. Sub executes work. Invoice arrives at Finance with no matching PO. Requires urgent manual approval from Moises/Samuel.
critical_pain_points.impact: Payments blocked. Subs frustrated. Financial risk from uncontrolled spending. Approval bottleneck at PM level.
critical_pain_points.detection_signal: More than 20% of invoices arriving without corresponding PO
critical_pain_points.solution: Mobile app requiring PO creation before authorizing any repair; supervisor cannot verbally approve without digital record

critical_pain_points.name: Checklist Done Without Proper Verification
critical_pain_points.description: Monitor marks checklist done (triggering sub payment) without thorough verification. Sub is paid and will NOT return to fix issues. New monitors are highest risk.
critical_pain_points.impact: Direct financial loss. Incomplete work paid for with no recourse. Single most financially dangerous handoff in the company.
critical_pain_points.detection_signal: Post-payment quality issues discovered during inspection; warranty claims on recently completed work
critical_pain_points.solution: Photo evidence + supervisor sign-off required before checklist done triggers payment

critical_pain_points.name: 12+ Simultaneous Spreadsheets for Purchasing
critical_pain_points.description: Purchasing team has 12+ spreadsheets with multiple tabs open daily. Similar information in different formats. Zorro, BT, and Excel all contain overlapping data with no integration.
critical_pain_points.impact: Extreme inefficiency. High probability of human error. Difficult onboarding for new team members.
critical_pain_points.detection_signal: Purchasing staff unable to answer simple questions without opening multiple spreadsheets
critical_pain_points.solution: Unified purchasing system with automatic cost allocation by house

critical_pain_points.name: No Notification When Inspection Hold Removed
critical_pain_points.description: When county places a hold on a house, there is no automatic notification when the hold is removed. Dandara must manually check county portal every single day.
critical_pain_points.impact: Houses sitting idle unnecessarily. Construction days lost waiting for a hold that was already removed.
critical_pain_points.detection_signal: Houses in hold status for longer than expected; manual daily county portal checks
critical_pain_points.solution: Automated county portal monitoring with push notification on hold status change

critical_pain_points.name: No Dedicated Warranty Software
critical_pain_points.description: No dedicated warranty management system. Using Builder Trend daily logs (3 per repair). One house had $14,000 in untracked warranty costs. Guarantees not properly tracked.
critical_pain_points.impact: Significant financial risk. Warranty periods unmonitored. Sub guarantee conflicts (sub counts 1 year from service end, company from CO date).
critical_pain_points.detection_signal: Warranty costs exceeding $5,000 per house; warranty claims discovered after sub guarantee period expired
critical_pain_points.solution: Dedicated warranty module with client portal, automatic guarantee tracking, and simplified repair workflow

critical_pain_points.name: Permit Expiration Without Warning (Marion County)
critical_pain_points.description: Marion County does not send email warnings before permit expiration (180 days). If permit expires, entire process restarts from zero. Manual tracking required.
critical_pain_points.impact: Permit cancellation = months of delay + re-application costs + construction halt.
critical_pain_points.detection_signal: Permits approaching 150 days without construction start; no automatic alerts in any system
critical_pain_points.solution: Automatic expiration tracking with alerts at 30/60/90/150 days before expiry

critical_pain_points.name: 3,500 Items Manual Update When Models Change
critical_pain_points.description: When a house model is updated, 3,500 takeoff items must be manually updated one by one. No template duplication or batch update mechanism exists.
critical_pain_points.impact: Days of manual work per model update. High probability of errors. Blocks budget and purchasing updates downstream.
critical_pain_points.detection_signal: Model updates taking more than 3 days; errors found in takeoff after budget already calculated
critical_pain_points.solution: Template system with duplication and batch update of values between similar models

critical_pain_points.name: Design Changes Not Communicated to Field
critical_pain_points.description: Floor plan revisions during construction are not systematically communicated to PMs and supervisors in the field. Construction may proceed with outdated plans.
critical_pain_points.impact: Construction errors. Costly rework. Legal liability for non-conforming construction.
critical_pain_points.detection_signal: Field reports of plan discrepancies; rework costs attributed to plan version errors
critical_pain_points.solution: Automatic notification of plan revision to all stakeholders; only active version visible in system

critical_pain_points.name: Draw Delays Block Construction Without Visibility
critical_pain_points.description: When financial draws are delayed, Scheduling has no visibility on when they will be released. House sits idle with sub crews potentially reassigned.
critical_pain_points.impact: Idle houses. Sub crews lost to competitors. No ability for Scheduling to plan around financial delays.
critical_pain_points.detection_signal: Houses with completed prerequisites but no draw released for >5 business days
critical_pain_points.solution: Real-time draw status dashboard visible to Scheduling with estimated release dates

critical_pain_points.name: Client Portal Schedule Outdated
critical_pain_points.description: Client portal in Builder Trend shows outdated schedule that does not account for holds, delays, or rescheduling. Clients see incorrect dates and file complaints.
critical_pain_points.impact: Client trust erosion. Frequent complaints. CR team spends significant time explaining discrepancies instead of productive work.
critical_pain_points.detection_signal: Client complaints about schedule accuracy; CR spending >2 hours/day on explanation calls
critical_pain_points.solution: Real-time portal update from actual construction management system

critical_pain_points.name: Impact Fees Block Final Electric in Marion
critical_pain_points.description: In Marion County, Final Electric inspection cannot proceed until Financeiro has paid impact fees. No cross-department visibility on this dependency. Inspection can be scheduled in vain.
critical_pain_points.impact: CO (Certificate of Occupancy) delayed. Wasted inspection scheduling. House delivery delayed.
critical_pain_points.detection_signal: Final Electric inspection failures due to unpaid impact fees; CO delays >2 weeks
critical_pain_points.solution: Automatic pre-check of impact fee status before scheduling Final Electric; alert to Financeiro when approaching inspection date

critical_pain_points.name: Takeoff Changes Not Notifying Budget
critical_pain_points.description: When takeoff quantities are updated, Budget team is not automatically notified. Budget may be calculated with outdated data, leading to incorrect material orders and cost estimates.
critical_pain_points.impact: Budget-reality divergence. Wrong materials purchased. Cost overruns discovered late.
critical_pain_points.detection_signal: Material orders not matching actual takeoff; budget variances >10% at house completion
critical_pain_points.solution: Automatic notification to Budget team when any takeoff is versioned; delta report of changes

critical_pain_points.name: Triple Registration for Utilities
critical_pain_points.description: Dandara enters the same utility data in three places: personal spreadsheet, Builder Trend daily logs, and BT custom fields. Each entry point has slightly different format.
critical_pain_points.impact: Significant daily rework. Inconsistencies between the three data sources. Error-prone process.
critical_pain_points.detection_signal: Dandara spending >1 hour/day on data entry duplication
critical_pain_points.solution: Single entry with automatic propagation to all required fields and reports

critical_pain_points.name: Manual PTO/Sick Day Tracking
critical_pain_points.description: HR manually tracks PTOs, sick days, accruals, and expirations using Google Calendar and spreadsheets. Daily calendar monitoring required. No automatic balance calculation.
critical_pain_points.impact: Hours weekly on manual tracking. Error-prone balance calculations. Leaders can schedule PTO without checking balance.
critical_pain_points.detection_signal: PTO balance errors discovered at payroll; retroactive corrections needed
critical_pain_points.solution: Automated HR module with real-time balance calculation, leader visibility, and calendar integration

critical_pain_points.name: Service Values Undefined at Execution Time
critical_pain_points.description: Core Craft teams execute services without knowing the cost. Yuri Melo defines values after execution. POs created retroactively. No price table for standard services.
critical_pain_points.impact: Financial rework. Retroactive PO creation. Budget accuracy impossible for Core Craft services.
critical_pain_points.detection_signal: POs created after service execution; Core Craft budget variances >15%
critical_pain_points.solution: Standard price table by service type; pre-approved value ranges; PO required before service execution

critical_pain_points.name: Inspection Hold Not Communicated Centrally
critical_pain_points.description: When an inspection places a hold on a house, the information is not communicated centrally. Scheduling may continue booking services. Client Relations may give wrong dates. Supervisors may not know.
critical_pain_points.impact: Wasted sub trips. Client misinformation. Work done on held houses that must be undone.
critical_pain_points.detection_signal: Services scheduled on houses with active holds; client complaints about conflicting status information
critical_pain_points.solution: Central hold dashboard visible to all departments; automatic blocking of scheduling for held houses
